title: ब्रह्मा गायत्री
devanagariText:
॥ श्री ब्रह्मा गायत्री ॥

ॐ चतुर्मुखाय विद्महे कमण्डलुधराय धीमहि।
तन्नो ब्रह्मा प्रचोदयात्॥

ॐ वेदात्मने विद्महे हिरण्यगर्भाय धीमहि।
तन्नो ब्रह्मा प्रचोदयात्॥

ॐ पद्मजाय विद्महे प्रजापतये धीमहि।
तन्नो ब्रह्मा प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ इति श्री ब्रह्मा गायत्री सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Brahma Gayatri ||

Om Chaturmukhaya Vidmahe Kamandaludharaya Dhimahi.
Tanno Brahma Prachodayat.

Om Vedatmane Vidmahe Hiranyagarbhaya Dhimahi.
Tanno Brahma Prachodayat.

Om Padmajaya Vidmahe Prajapataye Dhimahi.
Tanno Brahma Prachodayat.

|| Iti Shri Brahma Gayatri Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: हम चतुर्मुखी, कमण्डलुधारी ब्रह्मा जी का ध्यान करते हैं। वे वेदों की आत्मा, हिरण्यगर्भ हमारी बुद्धि को प्रेरित करें। हम कमल से उत्पन्न, प्रजापति ब्रह्मा का ध्यान करते हैं, वे हमें सृजन शक्ति और ज्ञान प्रदान करें।
benefits: Invokes blessings of Brahma, the creator god
Invokes the blessing of knowledge of vedas and scriptures
Invokes success in education and learning
Traditionally recited for removal of ignorance and awakens higher consciousness
description: Brahma Gayatri — ब्रह्मा गायत्री — is a powerful Vedic meditation mantra offered to Lord Brahma. This revered text originates from Vedic Tradition. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to remove ignorance and awaken higher consciousness as well as invoke blessings of Brahma, the creator god. Additionally, it is known to bestow knowledge of Vedas and scriptures. With 3 verses and a reading time of about 2 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Brahma Gayatri in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Brahma Gayatri Mantra in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred prayer to Lord Brahma for wisdom, creativity, and Vedic knowledge.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Brahma Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Brahma Gayatri (ब्रह्मा गायत्री) is the deity-specific (devata) Gayatri mantra dedicated to Lord Brahma, the four-faced creator (chaturmukha) of the universe and source of the four Vedas. It follows the standard Devata-Gayatri tripartite structure of Vidmahe (we know) — Dhimahi (we meditate upon) — Prachodayat (may that inspire us). The complete mantra has three vibhuti (epithet) variants: (1) Om Chaturmukhaya Vidmahe Kamandaludharaya Dhimahi, Tanno Brahma Prachodayat — invoking Brahma as the four-faced lord holding the kamandalu water-pot; (2) Om Vedatmane Vidmahe Hiranyagarbhaya Dhimahi, Tanno Brahma Prachodayat — invoking Brahma as the soul of the Vedas and the cosmic golden womb (Hiranyagarbha); and (3) Om Padmajaya Vidmahe Prajapataye Dhimahi, Tanno Brahma Prachodayat — invoking Brahma as the lotus-born Prajapati. (Source: classical Vedic Devata-Gayatri tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Brahma and why is he worshipped through this Gayatri?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Brahma is the first member of the Hindu Trimurti (Brahma-Vishnu-Mahesh), the Creator (Srishti-karta) of the universe, the four-faced (chaturmukha) lord who reveals the four Vedas (Rig, Yajur, Sama, Atharva) from his four mouths, and the Prajapati who fathered the Manasaputras (mind-born sons) — Marichi, Atri, Angiras, Pulastya, Pulaha, Kratu, Bhrigu, Daksha, Narada and Vasishtha. He is born from the lotus (padmaja) that arose from Vishnu's navel at the dawn of each kalpa, holds the kamandalu (water-pot of creative substance) and akshamala (rosary of time), and rides the Hamsa (swan of discrimination). Despite his cosmic supremacy, Brahma has only one major temple in India — the famous Brahma Mandir at Pushkar, Rajasthan — due to the curse of Sati / Sarasvati. The Brahma Gayatri is recited to invoke his blessings of creative power, Vedic learning and pure intellect. (Sources: Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Padma Purana; Skanda Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Chaturmukhaya Vidmahe Kamandaludharaya Dhimahi?
seoFaqs.answer: The first verse of the Brahma Gayatri breaks down as follows: Om — the Pranava, the supreme cosmic syllable; Chaturmukhaya — to the four-faced one (chatuh = four, mukha = face), Brahma's iconographic identifier as the lord with four heads facing the four directions; Vidmahe — may we know, may we comprehend; Kamandaludharaya — to the bearer (dhara) of the kamandalu (the ascetic water-pot containing the creative waters from which the universe arises); Dhimahi — may we meditate upon (deeply contemplate); Tanno Brahma — may that Brahma; Prachodayat — illumine, inspire, propel forward (our intellect). Total meaning: We seek to know the four-faced one, we meditate upon the bearer of the creative water-pot — may that Brahma inspire our minds to creative wisdom and Vedic understanding. (Source: classical Devata-Gayatri commentary tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Brahma Gayatri be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Brahma Gayatri is most powerfully recited at the three daily Sandhya periods (sunrise, noon, sunset) along with the standard Savitri Gayatri, and especially on Brahma Jayanti (Kartik Shukla Dwadashi), Akshaya Tritiya (the day Brahma began creation), Vasant Panchami (along with the Saraswati Gayatri), Sunday (Ravivar) and Wednesday (Budhvar). Students writing examinations, scholars beginning a new study, writers, poets, artists, architects, musicians and any creative professional should recite it 108 times daily before commencing their work. It is also recited as part of the Pancha-Devata upasana along with the Vishnu, Shiva, Devi and Ganesha Gayatris, before any new project, business venture or creative undertaking, and during Brahma-yajna (the daily duty of Vedic study) prescribed for every twice-born householder. (Sources: Devata-Gayatri vidhi; Brahma upasana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Brahma Gayatri grant according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition assigns the following blessings to sincere recitation of the Brahma Gayatri: (1) awakening of pure creative intelligence (medha-shakti, prajna) — the very faculty by which Brahma created the worlds; (2) success in all Vedic study and shastric scholarship; (3) blessings on every creative endeavour — writing, painting, music, architecture, scientific research, original innovation; (4) clarity of speech and powerful communication (vak-shakti, since Brahma is the consort of Saraswati Vagdevi); (5) successful ideation in business, planning and project management (Brahma is the original Prajapati / project-creator); (6) removal of intellectual confusion, mental fog and creative blocks; (7) Vedic-knowledge blessing for students of the four Vedas, six Vedangas and Upanishads; and (8) for the spiritual aspirant, awakening of the inner Hiranyagarbha — the cosmic golden seed of consciousness. (Source: Vedic Gayatri upasana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between the Brahma Gayatri and the universal Savitri Gayatri?
seoFaqs.answer: Both share the Gayatri metre (24 syllables in three padas of 8) but differ in deity, scriptural source and ritual function. The universal Gayatri or Savitri Gayatri (Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah, Tat Savitur Varenyam, Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi, Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat) is from Rigveda 3.62.10 by sage Vishvamitra and is addressed to Savitr — the Sun as the supreme awakener of cosmic intellect. It is the Veda-mata (mother of the Vedas), recited at every Sandhya by every twice-born Hindu, and is the universal Gayatri par excellence. The Brahma Gayatri is one of the twenty-four deity-specific (devata) Gayatris addressed to Brahma the Creator, with the standard Vidmahe-Dhimahi-Prachodayat structure but Brahma-specific epithets (Chaturmukha, Hiranyagarbha, Padmaja). Devotees recite Savitri Gayatri at every Sandhya as a duty and the Brahma Gayatri additionally for creative power and Vedic learning. (Source: Devata-Gayatri tradition; Rigveda 3.62.10.)
